title: Utah: Utah Consumer Privacy Act (UCPA) — narrower rights
summary: Utah residents can opt out of the sale of personal data and targeted advertising, and can delete data they provided to a business. Utah's deletion right is narrower than California's — it doesn't clearly reach data a business obtained from other sources — and Utah does not mandate a universal opt-out signal. The UCPA took effect on December 31, 2023.
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. Because Utah's deletion right is limited, the broker's own opt-out (below) is often the more effective route — verify before relying on either.
